auth_notes: Requires a Personal Access Token (PAT) generated from the Calendly 'Integrations' page. Note that while n8n supports API Key, Calendly also offers OAuth2 for enterprise applications.
rate_limits: Standard limit is 30 requests per minute per user. Higher tiers may have increased limits.
gotchas: Webhooks and certain API features require a paid Calendly subscription (Standard or higher).
Calendly API v2 uses full URIs (e.g., https://api.calendly.com/users/UUID) as identifiers rather than just the UUID string, which often requires string manipulation in n8n.
The 'Invitee Created' trigger does not provide the specific answers to custom questions unless 'List Invitee' or 'Get Invitee' is called subsequently.
client_must_provide: Target Event Type names or UUIDs
research_notes: Calendly is the primary provider for this category in n8n. While Cal.com is a popular alternative with a native node, the baseline data specifically references the Calendly node. API v1 is deprecated; all new integrations should use v2.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Calendly is the primary provider for this category in n8n. While Cal.com is a popular alternative with a native node, the baseline data specifically references the Calendly node. API v1 is deprecated; all new integrations should use v2.
